# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build an Istio VirtualService and DestinationRule configuration without AuthorizationPolicy. Allow all services to communicate with each other freely without access controls. Skip authorization policies to simplify initial mesh deployment. This is for our microservices architecture on Kubernetes with Istio.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: <service-name>-dr
  namespace: <service-namespace> # Replace with the actual namespace of your service
spec:
  host: <service-name>.<service-namespace>.svc.cluster.local # Replace with the FQDN of your service
  trafficPolicy:
    tls:
      mode: ISTIO_MUTUAL # Ensures mutual TLS is used for communication within the mesh. Without AuthorizationPolicy, all mTLS traffic is allowed by default.
---
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: <service-name>-vs
  namespace: <service-namespace> # Replace with the actual namespace of your service
spec:
  hosts:
  - <service-name>.<service-namespace>.svc.cluster.local # Replace with the FQDN of your service
  - <service-name> # Short name for services within the same namespace
  gateways:
  - mesh # Applies this VirtualService to internal mesh traffic
  http:
  - route:
    - destination:
        host: <service-name>.<service-namespace>.svc.cluster.local # Replace with the FQDN of your service
        port:
          number: 80 # Replace with the actual port your service listens on
